part.heading: Guide to, and ranking of, provisions

prov.heading: What this schedule does

para.text: This schedule contains provisions on the following matters:
the ranking of income provisions in and outside this schedule:
what, for the purposes of this Act, is income (including an overview of how child support may be income for the purposes of this Act):
general provisions on how income is calculated:
special provisions on how weekly income is calculated if that income is information share child support payments:
what may happen if an applicant for a benefit deprives the applicant of income, property, or both:
how insurance payments to or for a person affect liabilities for the purposes of various benefits.

history.history-note: Schedule 3 clause 1(b): replaced, on 1 July 2023, by section 38 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).
Schedule 3 clause 1(c): replaced, on 1 July 2023, by section 38 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).
Schedule 3 clause 1(ca): inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 38 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).

prov.heading: Ranking of provisions in and outside this schedule

para.text: Clause 3 is overridden by clauses 4 to 10.
Clauses 4 to 10 are overridden by any contrary provisions in this Act (for example, by regulations made under section 424(2)﻿(d), and by clause 16(3)).
Regulations made (under section 422) for the purposes of clause 9 override any contrary provisions in this Act.

part.heading: Income

prov.heading: General definition of income

para.text: Income, of a person, means the money value (before income tax) of a thing that—
is money received, or an interest acquired, by the person; and
is not an interest in capital received or acquired by the person.

cf.citation: 1964 No 136 s 3(1), definition of income, paragraph (a)

prov.heading: Income includes periodical payments, etc, for income-related purposes

para.text: A person’s income includes any periodical payments made to the person, and the value of any credits or services provided periodically to the person,—
from any source for income-related purposes; and
used by the person for income-related purposes.
Subclause (1) applies—
whether or not the payments, credits, or services are capital; and
to the payments, credits, or services as calculated before the deduction (if applicable) of income tax.

The contrary provisions that (under clause 2(2)) override this clause include clause 10 (which requires MSD to have regard to specified matters in considering, in respect of a person, whether a payment, credit, or service is for an income-related purpose).

cf.citation: 1964 No 136 s 3(1), definition of income, paragraph (b), and definition of income-related purpose

prov.heading: Income includes specified insurance payments

para.text: A person’s income includes the following payments made to the person:
any periodical income-related insurance payments:
any lump sum income-related insurance payment—
made in respect of the occurrence of a contingency; and
made within a period of 10 weeks from that occurrence; and
to the extent only that it replaces income lost by the person because of that occurrence.
Subclause (1) also applies to a payment referred to in subclause (1)﻿(a) or (b) that the person would have been entitled to receive—
under an accident insurance contract as defined in section 13 of the Accident Insurance Act 1998; and

but for the existence of a risk sharing agreement referred to in section 185 of the Accident Insurance Act 1998 (as it read immediately before its repeal by section 7 of the Accident Insurance Amendment Act 2000).
Subclause (1) applies—
whether or not the payments are capital; and
to the payments as calculated before the deduction (if applicable) of income tax.
The contrary provisions that (under clause 2(2)) override this clause include—
section 198 (loss of earnings compensation under Accident Compensation Act 2001); and
clause 10 (which requires MSD to have regard to specified matters in considering, in respect of a person, whether a payment, credit, or service is an income-related insurance payment).

cf.citation: 1964 No 136 s 3(1), definition of income, paragraph (c)

prov.heading: Income includes specified other payments related to work ending

para.text: A person’s income includes the following payments made to the person:
any payment relating to a situation involving a seasonal layoff:
any payment contingent on the completion of either a fixed-term engagement or an engagement to complete work specified in a contract:
any payment in lieu of notice terminating employment:

any payment (including holiday pay) that, if it had not been made in relation to termination of employment, would, in MSD’s opinion, have been paid so as to constitute monetary remuneration of the employee:
any parental leave payments paid under Part 7A of the Parental Leave and Employment Protection Act 1987:
any payment in relation to termination of employment made by a company pursuant to its constitution to any of its directors.

cf.citation: 1964 No 136 s 3(1), definition of income, paragraph (d)

prov.heading: Income includes value of goods, etc, supplied on regular basis

para.text: A person’s income includes the value of any goods, services, transport, or accommodation supplied on a regular basis to the person by any other person.
Subclause (1) applies to the value of the goods, services, transport, or accommodation as calculated before the deduction (if applicable) of income tax.

cf.citation: 1964 No 136 s 3(1), definition of income, paragraph (e)

prov.label: 7A
prov.heading: Overview of how child support may be income

para.text: Under clause 8(m), child support that is an interest acquired by a person (rather than money received by (a payment made to) a person) is not income of the person. Examples are—
an interest acquired that is an offset child support payment; or
an interest acquired by way of any child support debt recovery by reduced further payments of child support.
Under clause 8(n), child support that is a child support deduction for a receiving carer who is a UCB beneficiary is not income of that person.
Child support that is money received by a person is income of the person for the purposes of this Act unless it is—
para.label-para.crosshead: Court-ordered lump sums MSD determines are capital are not income

para.text: a payment that is ordered by the Family Court under section 109 of the Child Support Act 1991 (orders for provision of child support in form of lump sum) and that MSD determines, under clause 3(b) of this schedule, is an interest in capital received or acquired by the person; or

para.label-para.crosshead: Child support excess payments are not income

para.text: a child support excess payment (which includes, without limitation, a mixed child support payment) that, under clause 8(na), is not income of the person; or

para.label-para.crosshead: Payments covered by income exemption regulations are not income

para.text: a payment that, under clause 9 and income exemption regulations made under section 422, is not income of the person.
A child support payment is 1 of the following (as defined in Schedule 2)—
an information share child support payment; or
a general provisions child support payment.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: How information share child support payments are charged as income

para.text: An information share child support payment that is income is subject to—
general annual income provisions in Part 3 of this schedule (see clauses 12 and 14 (how annual income is calculated)); and
special weekly income provisions in Part 3A of this schedule.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: How general provisions child support payments are charged as income

para.text: A general provisions child support payment that is income is subject to—
general annual income provisions in Part 3 of this schedule (see clauses 12 and 14 (how annual income is calculated)); and
general weekly income provisions in Part 3 of this schedule (see clauses 13 and 14 (how weekly income is calculated)).

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Special provisions about cash assets or income for particular purposes

para.text: This overview is about income for the purposes of the Act, and so does not affect special provisions about cash assets or income for particular purposes, for example,—
accommodation supplement (see regulations made under section 423):
childcare assistance (see regulations made under section 424):
temporary additional support (see regulations made under section 428):
a benefit’s stand-down period (see regulations made under section 440).

history.history-note: Schedule 3 clause 7A: inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 39 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).

prov.heading: Income excludes specified benefits, payments, interests acquired, etc

para.text: A person’s income excludes the following amounts or payments:

any benefit, grant, allowance, or concession received by the person under this Act, Part 6 of the Veterans’ Support Act 2014, or Part 1 of the New Zealand Superannuation and Retirement Income Act 2001:
any pension or allowance (other than weekly income compensation or weekly compensation) under the Veterans’ Support Act 2014 received by any person because of the person’s own disablement:
any surviving spouse or partner pension received by the person under section 66 of the Veterans’ Support Act 2014:

any, or any part of any, pension or other periodical allowance received by any person from the Government of any Commonwealth country (other than New Zealand) that MSD determines is analogous to a pension or allowance specified in paragraph (b) or (c):
the value of any assistance of a kind approved by MSD provided to the person as a person with a health condition, injury, or disability to obtain or remain in employment:
any money received by the person by way of funeral benefit from any friendly society:
a participation allowance received by the person:
any money paid in respect of any military decoration and received by the person as a recipient of that decoration:

any money received by the person by way of an independence allowance under section 54 of the Accident Rehabilitation and Compensation Insurance Act 1992 or Part 13 or Part 4 of Schedule 1 of the Accident Insurance Act 1998, or any impairment lump sum received under Schedule 1 of the Accident Compensation Act 2001:

any money received by the person under the Oranga Tamariki Act 1989 in respect of the care of a child or young person (as those terms are defined in that Act), including financial assistance received by a young person (including a young adult), a caregiver, or other person under section 386AAG or 386B of that Act:
any income-related rent (within the meaning of the Public and Community Housing Management Act 1992):
any tax credit or amount received by the person under—
subparts MA to MG and MZ of the Income Tax Act 2007; or
subpart KD of the Income Tax Act 2004; or
subpart KD of the Income Tax Act 1994; or
Part 11A of the Income Tax Act 1976:
child support that is an interest acquired by the person, for example,—
an interest acquired that is an offset child support payment; or
an interest acquired by way of child support debt recovery by reduced further payments of child support:
a child support deduction for a receiving carer who is a UCB beneficiary:
a child support excess payment (which includes, without limitation, a mixed child support payment):
any amount of output tax charged in respect of a supply of goods and services made by that person:
any amount of goods and services tax payable by the Commissioner of Inland Revenue to that person:
if the person is an applicable person (as defined in section 2(1) of the Public and Community Housing Management Act 1992) in relation to any social housing,—
38% of the value of any goods or services provided or paid for—
by an additional resident; and
for any 1 or more of the reasons set out in paragraphs (a) to (c) of the definition of contributions in section 2(1) of the Public and Community Housing Management Act 1992:
38% of any payment made to the person—
by an additional resident; and
for any 1 or more of the reasons set out in paragraphs (a) to (c) of the definition of contributions in section 2(1) of the Public and Community Housing Management Act 1992:
38% of any amount that—
is received by the person from a boarder (as defined in section 65AAA) for board and lodgings at the premises at which the person resides; and
is not derived by the person from a business carried on by the person.
In addition, the income of a person who has accommodation income for a period excludes the person’s accommodation income for the period to the extent that it does not exceed,—
if the person resides at any social housing and is not an additional resident in relation to that housing, the market rent for that housing for the period; or
in any other case, the amount calculated in accordance with the following formula:
where—
is the person’s accommodation costs for the period
is,—
if the person does not use any separately identifiable part of the premises at which they reside primarily for business purposes during the period, zero; or

if paragraph (i) does not apply, the sum of the areas of each separately identifiable part of the premises at which the person resides that the person uses primarily for business purposes during the period
is,—
if, during the period, the person does not let any self-contained part of the premises at which they reside to another person or other persons who reside at those premises, zero; or

if paragraph (i) does not apply, the sum of the areas of each self-contained part of the premises at which the person resides that, during the period, the person lets to another person or other persons who reside at those premises
is,—
if, during the period, the person does not let any separately identifiable part of the premises at which they reside to another person or other persons who do not reside at those premises, zero; or

if paragraph (i) does not apply, the sum of the areas of each separately identifiable part of the premises at which the person resides that, during the period, the person lets to another person or other persons who do not reside at those premises
is the total area of the premises at which the person resides.
In this clause,—
accommodation costs has the meaning given to it by section 65AAA
accommodation income has the meaning given to it by clause 7 of Part 7 of Schedule 4
agency has the same meaning as in section 2(1) of the Public and Community Housing Management Act 1992
Kāinga Ora housing has the same meaning as in section 2(1) of the Public and Community Housing Management Act 1992
market rent,—
for Kāinga Ora housing and for a period, means the rent notified to the agency by Kāinga Ora–Homes and Communities—
as being the market rent for that housing; and
most recently before the agency last calculated or reviewed an income-related rent for a tenant of that housing before the start of the period:
for housing provided by a registered community housing provider and for a period, means the rent notified to the agency by the provider—
as being the market rent for that housing; and
most recently before the agency last calculated or reviewed an income-related rent for a tenant of that housing before the start of the period
tenant has the same meaning as in section 2(1) of the Public and Community Housing Management Act 1992.

cf.citation: 1964 No 136 s 3(1), definition of income, paragraph (f)﻿(i) to (xi), (xiii), (xvi), (xviii)

prov.label: 8A
prov.heading: Exception to exclusion in clause 8(a): income includes COVID-19 income relief payment

history.history-note: Schedule 3 clause 8A: repealed, on 11 February 2021, by clause 80(4) of Schedule 1.

prov.heading: Income excludes amounts, etc, declared not to be income

para.text: A person’s income excludes any amount, item, payment, or income from specified source, declared by regulations made under section 422 not to be income.

cf.citation: 1964 No 136 ss 3(1), definition of income, paragraph (f)﻿(xii), (xiv), (xv), (xvii), 3(3), 66, 66A, 66B, 68, Schedule 10

prov.heading: Considerations for whether income-related insurance payment or income-related purpose

para.text: In considering, in respect of a person, whether a payment, credit, or service is an income-related insurance payment or (as the case may be) is for an income-related purpose, MSD must have regard to the following matters:
the nature of the contingency in respect of which the payment, credit, or service was made, provided, or used:
the nature of the payment, credit, or service:
the manner in which, and the occasions on which, the payment, credit, or service is made, provided, or used:
the manner in which the right to or eligibility for the payment, credit, or service is expressed in any contract of insurance or in any other document:
whether the payment, credit, or service is required to be made or provided to any other person and, if so, the relationship between the persons:
whether the person has assigned the person’s right to receive the payment, credit, or service:
any other matters that MSD considers, in the circumstances of the particular case, to be relevant.

cf.citation: 1964 No 136 s 3(1), definition of income, paragraphs (b) and (f)﻿(xiv)﻿(C), definition of income-related insurance payment, s 3(4)

part.heading: General provisions on calculation of income

history.history-note: Schedule 3 Part 3 heading: amended, on 1 July 2023, by section 41 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).

prov.heading: Interpretation

para.text: In clauses 12 to 14,—
appropriate number of weeks means a number that—
must not exceed 52; and
is specified, for the benefit concerned, in regulations made under section 418(1)﻿(o)
specified provision means a provision that is all or a portion of legislation in or made under all or any of the following:
this Act (for example, a schedule of this Act):
the New Zealand Superannuation and Retirement Income Act 2001:
Part 6 of the Veterans’ Support Act 2014.

history.history-note: Schedule 3 clause 11 specified provision: amended, on 1 July 2023, by section 42 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).

prov.heading: How annual income is calculated

para.text: A person’s annual income, for the rate of benefit, or as referred to in a specified provision, is (unless the context otherwise requires) the person’s estimated income for the 52 weeks commencing on the date on which the benefit (if granted, or regranted) will commence.

The estimated annual income for the 52 weeks specified in subclause (1) is an amount equal to the income received by the person for the 52 weeks ending on the day preceding the date on which the benefit, if granted or regranted, will commence as MSD determines.

The income received by the person for the 52 weeks specified in subclause (2) is or includes any information share child support payment that is charged as weekly income of the person under Part 3A of this schedule for all or any of those weeks.

history.history-note: Schedule 3 clause 12 heading: replaced, on 1 July 2023, by section 43(1) of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).
Schedule 3 clause 12(3): inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 43(2) of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).

prov.heading: How weekly income is calculated: general

para.text: A person’s weekly income, for the rate of benefit, or as referred to in a specified provision, is (unless the context otherwise requires) the person’s weekly income calculated by dividing the person’s total income over the appropriate number of weeks by that number of weeks.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Periods to which income (other than annual holiday pay income) relates

para.text: MSD must, in calculating a person’s weekly income under subclause (1), determine the period or periods to which any income (other than annual holiday pay income) relates based only on—
the extent to which it was earned in that period or those periods; or
the extent to which any other entitlement to it arose in, or in respect of, that period or those periods.

text.emphasis: Period based only on when entitlement to income arose

para.text: A person (P) receives income that is a lump sum of weekly loss of earnings compensation (as defined in section 198(3)).
P’s entitlement to the compensation arose in, or in respect of, a period some or all of which is earlier than the following times:
the time at which P established the claim for the compensation:
the time at which P received the compensation.
MSD must determine the period or periods to which the income relates based only on the extent to which P’s entitlement to it arose in, or in respect of, that period or those periods.

If MSD cannot determine the period or periods to which any income relates under subclause (1A), MSD may, in calculating a person’s weekly income under subclause (1), determine the period or periods to which any income relates having regard to the period or periods for which it was otherwise received, acquired, paid, provided, or supplied.

MSD’s determination under subclause (1A) or (2) is not limited to, or by, the exact time or times of the relevant earning, entitlement, receipt, acquisition, payment, provision, or supply referred to in that subclause﻿.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Periods to which annual holiday pay income relates

para.text: MSD must, in calculating a person’s weekly income under subclause (1), determine the period or periods to which annual holiday pay income relates based only on the extent to which, in that period or those periods, the person does, or is treated as having done, any of the 3 things set out in subclause (3B), (3C), or (3D).

The first thing is that the person takes the annual holidays concerned during their employment (regardless of whether and, if so, when the person is paid, and receives, the annual holiday pay income).
The second thing is that the person receives the annual holiday pay income, if—
the person does not take the annual holidays concerned during their employment; and
the annual holiday pay income is paid to and received by the person before the time that their employment ends; and
the annual holiday pay income is not paid to and received by the person in relation to termination of their employment.
The third thing is that the person is under paragraph (c) of this subclause treated as having taken the annual holidays concerned, if—
the person does not take the annual holidays concerned during their employment; and
the annual holiday pay income is paid to and received by the person—
after the time that their employment ends; or
before or at the time that their employment ends and in relation to termination of their employment; and therefore
this paragraph requires the person to be treated for the purposes only of subclause (3A) as if they had taken the annual holidays concerned starting on the first week day after their employment ends.
In subclause (3D)﻿(c), a week day means a day—
that is a day of the week other than a Saturday or a Sunday; and
whether or not a public holiday falls on that day.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: This clause does not apply to information share child support payments

para.text: Nothing in this clause applies to any information share child support payment (see Part 3A of this schedule).

history.history-note: Schedule 3 clause 13 heading: replaced, on 1 July 2023, by section 44(1) of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).
Schedule 3 clause 13(1) heading: inserted, on 11 March 2026, by section 7(1) of the Social Security (Accident Compensation and Calculation of Weekly Income) Amendment Act 2026 (2026 No 6).
Schedule 3 clause 13(1A) heading: inserted, on 11 March 2026, by section 7(2) of the Social Security (Accident Compensation and Calculation of Weekly Income) Amendment Act 2026 (2026 No 6).
Schedule 3 clause 13(1A): inserted, on 11 March 2026, by section 7(2) of the Social Security (Accident Compensation and Calculation of Weekly Income) Amendment Act 2026 (2026 No 6).
Schedule 3 clause 13(2): replaced, on 11 March 2026, by section 7(2) of the Social Security (Accident Compensation and Calculation of Weekly Income) Amendment Act 2026 (2026 No 6).
Schedule 3 clause 13(3): replaced, on 11 March 2026, by section 7(2) of the Social Security (Accident Compensation and Calculation of Weekly Income) Amendment Act 2026 (2026 No 6).
Schedule 3 clause 13(3A) heading: inserted, on 11 March 2026, by section 7(2) of the Social Security (Accident Compensation and Calculation of Weekly Income) Amendment Act 2026 (2026 No 6).
Schedule 3 clause 13(3A): inserted, on 11 March 2026, by section 7(2) of the Social Security (Accident Compensation and Calculation of Weekly Income) Amendment Act 2026 (2026 No 6).
Schedule 3 clause 13(3B): inserted, on 11 March 2026, by section 7(2) of the Social Security (Accident Compensation and Calculation of Weekly Income) Amendment Act 2026 (2026 No 6).
Schedule 3 clause 13(3C): inserted, on 11 March 2026, by section 7(2) of the Social Security (Accident Compensation and Calculation of Weekly Income) Amendment Act 2026 (2026 No 6).
Schedule 3 clause 13(3D): inserted, on 11 March 2026, by section 7(2) of the Social Security (Accident Compensation and Calculation of Weekly Income) Amendment Act 2026 (2026 No 6).
Schedule 3 clause 13(3E): inserted, on 11 March 2026, by section 7(2) of the Social Security (Accident Compensation and Calculation of Weekly Income) Amendment Act 2026 (2026 No 6).
Schedule 3 clause 13(4) heading: inserted, on 11 March 2026, by section 7(2) of the Social Security (Accident Compensation and Calculation of Weekly Income) Amendment Act 2026 (2026 No 6).
Schedule 3 clause 13(4): inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 44(2) of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).

prov.heading: Adjustments for likely reductions or increases

para.text: The estimated income for the period for which the calculation under clause 12(1) or 13(1) is made is the income as calculated under clause 12(1) or 13(1), but adjusted for all (if any) of the following deductions and additions:
MSD may deduct any items by which MSD is satisfied that income as so calculated is likely to be reduced:
MSD may add any items by which MSD is satisfied that income as so calculated is likely to be increased.

prov.heading: Rules on how MSD determines income of people engaged in business or trade

para.text: The Governor-General may, by Order in Council, make rules—
prescribing how MSD is to assess and determine for the purposes of this Act the income of all or any of the following:
self-employed people:
people carrying on business in a partnership:
people trading as a small business:
directors of a company:
people carrying on business or trade through a company of which they are—
a director and a shareholder; or
an employee and a shareholder; or
a director, a shareholder, and an employee:
people otherwise engaged in business or trade; and
prescribing the circumstances in which those rules apply.
However, neither subclause (1) nor rules made under subclause (1) apply to any information share child support payment (see Part 3A of this schedule).
Rules made under subclause (1) may prescribe for the purposes of this Act all or any of the following:
the kinds of deduction from income allowed under the Income Tax Act 2007 that are to be disregarded in determining income, and—
the circumstances in which the deductions are to be disregarded; and
whether the deductions are to be disregarded wholly or in part only:
how income is to be calculated if business accounts are presented in cash or accrual form:
livestock valuation methods, and their treatment, in calculating income:
the extent (if any) to which, and circumstances in which, all or any of the following are to be treated as a person’s income:
the person’s drawings from a business or trade:
goods or services supplied to the person by a business or trade:
distributions to the person from a business or trade:
income from a business or trade by the person:
how it is to be determined whether a person has used assets of a business or trade for no consideration or inadequate consideration:
how a person’s income is to be determined if the person has used assets of a business or trade for no consideration or inadequate consideration:
the extent (if any) to which depreciation or other provision for replacement of capital assets is to be—
allowed as a deduction from income; or
disregarded in calculating income:
the extent (if any) to which, and circumstances in which, either or both of the following are to be treated as income:
distributions from a company:
retained profits of a company:
the treatment of look-through companies (within the meaning of the Income Tax Act 2007).
Subclause (2)﻿(d) applies to businesses and trades however they are carried on.
Subclause (3) does not limit subclause (1).
Rules made under subclause (1), if those rules reduce the income of people, have effect in respect of any income specified in the rules on and after a date that is—
specified in the rules; and
earlier than, the same as, or later than, the date on which the rules are made.
Rules under this clause are secondary legislation (see Part 3 of the Legislation Act 2019 for publication requirements).

legtable.summary: The following table is small in size and has 3 columns that are grouped under the heading Legislation Act 2019 requirements for secondary legislation made under this clause.

entry.emphasis: Legislation Act 2019 requirements for secondary legislation made under this clause

row.entry: PCO must publish it on the legislation website and notify it in the Gazette
The Minister must present it to the House of Representatives
It may be disallowed by the House of Representatives

entry.emphasis: This note is not part of the Act.

history.history-note: Schedule 3 clause 15(1A): inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 45 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).
Schedule 3 clause 15(6): inserted, on 28 October 2021, by section 3 of the Secondary Legislation Act 2021 (2021 No 7).

part.label: 3A
part.heading: Special provisions on calculation of weekly income that is information share child support payment

history.history-note: Schedule 3 Part 3A: inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 46 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).

part.crosshead: How payment is charged as weekly income

history.history-note: Heading: inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 46 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).

prov.label: 15A
prov.heading: How payment is charged as weekly income

para.text: This Part sets out how a person’s information share child support payment is all or any of their weekly income—
for the rate of benefit; or
for a specified provision (as defined in clause 15C).

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Key steps in charging payment as weekly income

para.text: The key steps in charging the payment as weekly income are as follows:
the payment is taken to be received at deemed receipt:
deemed receipt is used to identify when income charging starts and ends:
the payment amount is divided by the number of weeks the payment is charged to produce equal amounts of weekly income for each of those weeks.

Under the exception in clause 15H (terminal benefit), which applies when the person has died, and their benefit will end, or has ended, under section 326(2) or (3), the payment is not charged as the person’s weekly income.
Under the exception in clause 15I (transfer to new benefit paid in different instalments), MSD may charge the payment amount—
as income for other weeks; and
as equal or unequal amounts of weekly income for each of those other weeks.

history.history-note: Schedule 3 clause 15A: inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 46 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).

prov.label: 15B
prov.heading: Effect of non-payment of benefit or other assistance

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Part applies even if benefit or other assistance not payable

para.text: This Part applies to a person and an income-charging period even if the person’s benefit or other assistance under this Act is not payable at all or any of the following times:
at or after the time of disclosure to MSD of a payment that could be charged as income in the period:
at the start or end of, or at any other time or times in, the period.

example.heading: Examples of benefit or other assistance not payable

para.text: The person’s benefit or other assistance under this Act—
is not payable at the start of the period; but
is payable again later in the period.
The person’s benefit or other assistance under this Act—
is payable at the start of the period; but
is not payable, then is payable again, later in the period.
Subclause (1) applies whether or not the person’s benefit or other assistance under this Act changes at the start or end of, or at any other time or times in, the period.
But subclause (1)﻿(a) does not apply (despite subclause (4)) to a person’s benefit or other assistance under this Act that is not payable because it has been cancelled.
For the purposes of subclause (1), a person’s benefit or other assistance under this Act is not payable (subject to subclause (3)) if all or any of the following apply:

the benefit or other assistance under this Act is, in connection with a review under section 304 or otherwise, suspended, or otherwise not payable, in connection with decisions MSD has made, or is to make, about whether the person is, or remains, entitled to—
the benefit or other assistance under this Act; or
the rate of benefit or other assistance under this Act that was paid to the person:

the person is subject to legislation in or made under this Act that provides for the suspension, or other non-payment, in certain circumstances, of that person’s benefit or other assistance under this Act.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Legislation providing for suspension or other non-payment

para.text: The legislation mentioned in subclause (4)﻿(b) includes, without limitation,—
section 213 (immediate suspension of benefit at request of New Zealand Police):
section 219 (general rule: benefit not payable while beneficiary absent from New Zealand):
section 237 (sanction for second failure: suspension of main benefit):
section 270 (sanction for first or second failure: suspension of in-hand allowance and incentive payments):
section 280 (sanction for first or second failure: suspension of in-hand allowance and incentive payments):
sections 331 to 336 (expiry and regrant of specified benefits):
regulations made under section 436 (regulations: factors affecting benefits: absence from New Zealand):
regulations made under section 441(1) (regulations: expiry and regrant of benefits).

history.history-note: Schedule 3 clause 15B: inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 46 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).
Heading: inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 46 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).

prov.label: 15C
prov.heading: Interpretation

para.text: In this Part,—
business day, for an information share child support payment, means a day of the week other than—
a Saturday, a Sunday, Waitangi Day, Good Friday, Easter Monday, Anzac Day, the Sovereign’s birthday, Te Rā Aro ki a Matariki/Matariki Observance Day, and Labour Day; and
if Waitangi Day or Anzac Day falls on a Saturday or a Sunday, the following Monday; and
Christmas Day, Boxing Day, New Year’s Day, and 2 January; and
if Christmas Day or 1 January falls on a Friday, the following Monday; and
if Christmas Day or 1 January falls on a Saturday or a Sunday, the following Monday and Tuesday
expected date of the next in-cycle payment, for an in-cycle payment made on the second business day after the 19th day of a month, means the third business day after the 19th day of the next month
in-cycle payment means an information share child support payment that is made—
by the Commissioner of Inland Revenue to the receiving carer; and
on the second business day after the 19th day of a month
out-of-cycle payment means an information share child support payment that is not an in-cycle payment
specified provision has the same meaning as in clause 11 of this schedule
standard income-charging period means,—
if the relevant benefit or other assistance is paid in instalments of 1 week’s benefit or other assistance, a period that—
starts on a Monday; and
ends at the close of the first Sunday after that Monday; and
if the relevant benefit or other assistance is paid in instalments of 2 weeks’ benefit or other assistance, a period that—
starts on a Wednesday; and
ends at the close of the second Tuesday after that Wednesday

transaction date, for an information share child support payment to a person, means the date of the business day on which a bank is to make the payment to the person by direct credit to a bank account nominated by the person under section 148 of the Child Support Act 1991, as the date of that business day is—
stated in the Commissioner of Inland Revenue’s instructions to the bank; and
disclosed to MSD under the specified approved information sharing agreement.

history.history-note: Schedule 3 clause 15C: inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 46 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).
Heading: inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 46 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).

prov.label: 15D
prov.heading: Deemed receipt: general rule

para.text: In this Part, the deemed receipt of a person’s information share child support payment is,—
at the start of the first business day after the transaction date, if MSD makes no determination under clause 15E; or
if MSD makes a determination under clause 15E, at the start of the date determined under that clause.

history.history-note: Schedule 3 clause 15D: inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 46 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).

prov.label: 15E
prov.heading: Deemed receipt: exception

para.text: MSD may determine that a person’s information share child support payment is, for the purposes of this Part, taken to be received—
not at the start of the first business day after the transaction date (under the general rule in clause 15D(a)); but instead
at the start of a later date.

MSD must consider the day on which the person actually received the payment, and any advantage to the person of the general rule in clause 15D(a) not applying to the person, if, and when, MSD decides—
whether to make a determination under this clause; and
the terms of a determination made under this clause.

history.history-note: Schedule 3 clause 15E: inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 46 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).

part.crosshead: Payment charged as income: general rules

history.history-note: Heading: inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 46 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).

prov.label: 15F
prov.heading: When income charging starts: general rule

para.text: For a person’s in-cycle payment or out-of-cycle payment, income charging starts as set out in item 1 or 2 of the following table:

legtable.summary: The following table is small in size and has 3 columns. Column 1 is headed Item. Column 2 is headed If the relevant benefit or other assistance is paid. Column 3 is headed income charging starts.

entry.emphasis: If the relevant benefit or other assistance is paid ...

row.entry: in instalments of 1 week’s benefit or other assistance

para.text: at the start of a standard income-charging period, if deemed receipt falls in that period

row.entry: in instalments of 2 weeks’ benefit or other assistance

para.text: at the start of a standard income-charging period, if deemed receipt falls in week 1 of that period
at the start of the next standard income-charging period, if deemed receipt falls in week 2 of a standard income-charging period

history.history-note: Schedule 3 clause 15F: inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 46 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).

prov.label: 15G
prov.heading: When income charging ends: general rule

para.text: For a person’s in-cycle payment, income charging ends as set out in item 1 or 2 of the following table:

legtable.summary: The following table is small in size and has 3 columns. Column 1 is headed Item. Column 2 is headed If the relevant benefit or other assistance is paid. Column 3 is headed income charging ends.

entry.emphasis: If the relevant benefit or other assistance is paid ...

row.entry: in instalments of 1 week’s benefit or other assistance

para.text: at the end of the standard income-charging period immediately before the expected date of the next in-cycle payment

row.entry: in instalments of 2 weeks’ benefit or other assistance

para.text: at the end of the 4 weeks that start on the day on which the payment starts to be charged as the person’s income
For a person’s out-of-cycle payment, income charging ends as set out in item 1 or 2 of the following table:

legtable.summary: The following table is small in size and has 3 columns. Column 1 is headed Item. Column 2 is headed If the relevant benefit or other assistance is paid. Column 3 is headed income charging ends.

entry.emphasis: If the relevant benefit or other assistance is paid ...

row.entry: in instalments of 1 week’s benefit or other assistance

para.text: at the end of the first Sunday before the day that—

row.entry: (a) is in the month after the month of deemed receipt; and
(b) corresponds to the day of deemed receipt
at the end of the first day of the second month after the month of deemed receipt, if the month after the month of deemed receipt contains no day that corresponds to the day of deemed receipt
in instalments of 2 weeks’ benefit or other assistance

para.text: at the end of the 4 weeks that start on the day on which the payment starts to be charged as the person’s income

history.history-note: Schedule 3 clause 15G: inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 46 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).
Heading: inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 46 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).

prov.label: 15H
prov.heading: Terminal benefit

para.text: This clause applies to the person’s information share child support payment (whether an in-cycle payment or an out-of-cycle payment) if—
the person has died, and their benefit—
is specified in any of paragraphs (a) to (h) of section 326(1); and
will end, or has ended, under section 326(2) or (3); and
the payment’s deemed receipt is after the person’s death.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Payment is not charged as weekly income

para.text: The payment is not charged as the person’s weekly income.
This clause overrides clauses 15F and 15G.

history.history-note: Schedule 3 clause 15H: inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 46 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).

prov.label: 15I
prov.heading: Transfer to new benefit paid in different instalments

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Transfer from weekly-instalment benefit to fortnightly-instalment benefit

para.text: This clause applies to a person who transfers—
from a benefit or other assistance that is payable in instalments of 1 week’s benefit or other assistance; and
to a benefit or other assistance that is payable in instalments of 2 weeks’ benefit or other assistance.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Transfer from fortnightly-instalment benefit to weekly-instalment benefit

para.text: This clause also applies to a person who transfers—
from a benefit or other assistance that is payable in instalments of 2 weeks’ benefit or other assistance; and
to a benefit or other assistance that is payable in instalments of 1 week’s benefit or other assistance.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: MSD may determine that payment is charged as income for other weeks

para.text: MSD may determine that the person’s information share child support payment is charged as the person’s weekly income for a period that—
MSD identifies; and
is after the deemed receipt; and
is 3 or more weeks, but not more than 6 weeks; and
does not start or end as required by clauses 15F and 15G.
In making a determination under this clause, MSD may charge the payment amount as equal or unequal amounts of weekly income.
MSD must consider how MSD can best charge the payment as weekly income reasonably and conveniently if, and when, MSD decides—
whether to make a determination under this clause; and
the terms of a determination made under this clause.
This clause overrides clauses 15F and 15G.

history.history-note: Schedule 3 clause 15I: inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 46 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).

part.heading: Deprivation of income or property

prov.heading: Effect of deprivation of income or property

para.text: This clause applies if MSD is satisfied that—
deprivation of 1 or both of the following kinds has occurred:
an applicant for a benefit has, directly or indirectly, deprived the applicant of income, property, or both:
the spouse or partner of an applicant for a benefit has, directly or indirectly, deprived the spouse or partner of income, property, or both; and
the deprivation results in the applicant’s qualifying for that benefit, any other benefit, or an increased rate of benefit.

For the purposes of this clause and of rules under clause 17, deprivation of income, property, or both, by a person who is an applicant for a benefit, or the spouse or partner of that applicant, excludes that person doing all or any of—
not applying for a formula assessment of child support payable in respect of a qualifying child under the Child Support Act 1991:
electing to end a formula assessment of child support payable in respect of a qualifying child under that Act:
choosing not to enter into a voluntary agreement for child support in respect of a qualifying child under that Act (whether or not that voluntary agreement—
is a voluntary agreement that, under section 48 of that Act, qualifies for acceptance by the Commissioner of Inland Revenue; and so
is a qualifying voluntary agreement (as defined in section 2(1) of that Act)):
making under that Act an election to terminate liability under a qualifying voluntary agreement that, under section 48 of that Act, has been accepted by the Commissioner of Inland Revenue:

not making, or making, any other arrangement (for example, one in respect of a qualifying child and that is not a qualifying voluntary agreement (as those terms are defined in section 2(1) of that Act)) that does or may affect whether or what child support is payable in respect of a qualifying child under that Act.
Subclause (1A) does not apply to, and does not limit the application of section 9 (UCB beneficiaries must apply for formula assessment) of the Child Support Act 1991 to,—
an applicant for an unsupported child’s benefit; or
the spouse or partner of that applicant; or
a UCB beneficiary; or
the spouse or partner of that UCB beneficiary.
MSD may refuse to grant a benefit, cancel or reduce a benefit already granted, or grant a benefit at a reduced rate.
In this clause and clause 17,—
income excludes, for 12 months after the date of the person’s receipt of the amount, an amount that—
MSD considers is, or might have been, derived by a person from—
an impairment lump sum under Schedule 1 of the Accident Compensation Act 2001; or
a lump sum payment of an independence allowance under Part 13 or Part 4 of Schedule 1 of the Accident Insurance Act 1998; and
is paid to, and received by, the person, after (when section 74(2) of the Social Security Act 1964 commenced at) the close of 31 March 2002
property excludes, for 12 months after the date of the person’s receipt of the sum or payment, a sum or payment that—
was paid to, and received by, a person as—
an impairment lump sum under Schedule 1 of the Accident Compensation Act 2001; or
a lump sum payment of an independence allowance under Part 13 or Part 4 of Schedule 1 of the Accident Insurance Act 1998; and
is paid to, and received by, the person, after (when section 74(2) of the Social Security Act 1964 commenced at) the close of 31 March 2002.
This clause—
overrides any provisions to the contrary in this Act (under clause 2(3)), Part 6 of the Veterans’ Support Act 2014, or the New Zealand Superannuation and Retirement Income Act 2001; but
is subject to any rules prescribed by rules under clause 17.

history.history-note: Schedule 3 clause 16(1A): inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 47 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).
Schedule 3 clause 16(1B): inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 47 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).

prov.heading: Rules on how MSD determines deprivation of income or property

para.text: The Governor-General may, by Order in Council, for the purposes of clause 16 make rules—
relating to the deprivation of income, property, or both; and
prescribing the circumstances in which the rules apply.

Rules under this clause are subject to clause 16(1A) (under which deprivation of income, property, or both, excludes specified conduct by a person who is an applicant for a benefit, or the spouse or partner of that applicant).
Rules under this clause are secondary legislation (see Part 3 of the Legislation Act 2019 for publication requirements).

legtable.summary: The following table is small in size and has 3 columns that are grouped under the heading Legislation Act 2019 requirements for secondary legislation made under this clause.

entry.emphasis: Legislation Act 2019 requirements for secondary legislation made under this clause

row.entry: PCO must publish it on the legislation website and notify it in the Gazette
The Minister must present it to the House of Representatives
It may be disallowed by the House of Representatives

entry.emphasis: This note is not part of the Act.

history.history-note: Schedule 3 clause 17(1A): inserted, on 1 July 2023, by section 48 of the Child Support (Pass On) Acts Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 27).
Schedule 3 clause 17(2): inserted, on 28 October 2021, by section 3 of the Secondary Legislation Act 2021 (2021 No 7).

part.heading: Effect on liabilities of insurance payments

prov.heading: How accommodation costs affected by debt insurance payment in relation to mortgage security

para.text: This clause applies to a person if a debt insurance payment in relation to a mortgage security is made to the person, or to some other person—
on the person’s behalf; or
for the person’s benefit.

For the purposes of the definition in section 65AAA of accommodation costs, the payments required to be made by that person under that mortgage security are taken to be reduced by the amount of the debt insurance payment.

history.history-note: Schedule 3 clause 18(2): amended, on 2 March 2026, by section 14(3) of the Social Assistance Legislation (Accommodation Supplement and Income-related Rent) Amendment Act 2025 (2025 No 27).

prov.heading: How expenses for disability allowance may be affected by health or disability insurance payment

para.text: This clause applies to a person if a health or disability insurance payment is made to the person, or to some other person—
on the person’s behalf; or
for the person’s benefit; or
on behalf of a member of the person’s family; or
for the benefit of a member of the person’s family.

For the purposes of subpart 14 of Part 2 (disability allowance), any additional expenses of the person arising from the person’s disability, or expenses arising from the hospitalisation of the person’s spouse or partner, as the case may be, are taken to be reduced to the extent, if any, MSD considers appropriate.
MSD must, in determining the extent of any reduction, have regard to the nature of the payment and those additional expenses or expenses.

prov.heading: How commitments for temporary additional support may be affected by debt, or health or disability, insurance payment

para.text: This clause applies to a person if a debt insurance payment, or a health or disability insurance payment, is made to the person, or to some other person—
on the person’s behalf; or
for the person’s benefit; or
on behalf of a member of the person’s family; or
for the benefit of a member of the person’s family.
For the purposes only of temporary additional support, the person’s commitments are taken to be reduced to the extent, if any, MSD considers appropriate.
MSD must, in determining the extent of any reduction, have regard to the nature of the payment and of those commitments.
In this clause, temporary additional support means either or both of the following:
temporary additional support under subpart 16 of Part 2:
a special benefit continued under section 23 of the Social Security (Working for Families) Amendment Act 2004 (as that section is saved by clause 19 of Schedule 1 of this Act).

prov.heading: How costs, etc, for approved programme of special assistance may be affected by debt, or health or disability, insurance payment

para.text: This clause applies to a person if a debt insurance payment, or a health or disability insurance payment, is made to the person, or to some other person—
on the person’s behalf; or
for the person’s benefit; or
on behalf of a member of the person’s family; or
for the benefit of a member of the person’s family.

For the purposes of any approved special assistance programme under section 101, the costs, expenses, and commitments of the person are taken to be reduced to the extent, if any, MSD considers appropriate having regard to the nature of the payment and those costs, expenses, and commitments.

history.history-note: Schedule 4 heading: amended, on 22 February 2024, by section 8 of the Social Security (Benefits Adjustment) and Income Tax (Minimum Family Tax Credit) Amendment Act 2024 (2024 No 3).

part.heading: Jobseeker support

legtable.summary: The following table is small in size and has 4 columns. The columns have no headings.

row.entry: To a single beneficiary under the age of 20 years who is—
$276.46 a week, subject to Income Test 3
living with a parent (as that term is defined in clause 8); and
whose benefit commenced on or after 1 July 1998
To any other single beneficiary under the age of 25 years without dependent children (see clause 7)
$324.50 a week, subject to Income Test 3

To any other single beneficiary without dependent children and who was on 15 July 2013 transferred to jobseeker support by clause 2(9) and (11), or by clause 10(6) and (8), of Schedule 32 of the Social Security Act 1964
$384.52 a week, subject to Income Test 1
To any other single beneficiary without dependent children
$372.55 a week, subject to Income Test 3

To any single beneficiary whose youngest dependent child is aged at least 14 years (including, without limitation, any such beneficiary who is a beneficiary of a kind specified in clause 4(a), (b), or (c), and see also MSD's discretion (to disregard specified income used to meet the cost of childcare) under clause 5)
$521.52 a week, subject to Income Test 1
To any other single beneficiary with 1 or more dependent children
$521.52 a week, subject to Income Test 1
To a beneficiary who is in a relationship whose spouse or partner is granted, in the spouse’s or partner’s own right, a main benefit under this Act
$316.97 a week subject to Income Test 4 if the beneficiary and his or her spouse or partner do not have 1 or more dependent children; or
$334.70 a week subject to Income Test 4 if the beneficiary and his or her spouse or partner have 1 or more dependent children
To a beneficiary who is in a relationship whose spouse or partner is granted, in the spouse’s or partner’s own right, New Zealand superannuation or a veteran’s pension
$316.97 a week subject to Income Test 4 if the beneficiary and his or her spouse or partner do not have 1 or more dependent children; or
$334.70 a week subject to Income Test 4 if the beneficiary and his or her spouse or partner have 1 or more dependent children

To a beneficiary who is in a relationship whose spouse or partner is not granted a main benefit under this Act, New Zealand superannuation, or a veteran’s pension, in the spouse’s or partner’s own right
$633.94 a week subject to Income Test 3 if the beneficiary and his or her spouse or partner do not have 1 or more dependent children; or
$669.40 a week subject to Income Test 3 if the beneficiary and his or her spouse or partner have 1 or more dependent children
Maximum increase in respect of a housekeeper

Special rate of benefit (and a rate to be received by the person during the period of non-entitlement of the person’s spouse or partner) for a person who has a spouse or partner who is ineligible for a benefit for a period because of—
$316.97 a week subject to Income Test 4 if the beneficiary does not have 1 or more dependent children; or
the application of sections 225 to 229 (which relate to voluntary unemployment or loss of employment through misconduct, etc); or
$334.70 a week subject to Income Test 4 if the beneficiary has 1 or more dependent children
the application of sections 234 to 238 (which relate to sanctions that may be imposed for failures to comply with work-test or other obligations and work-preparation interviews and exercises); or
a strike, either by the spouse or partner, or by fellow members of the same union at the same place of employment
A single beneficiary whose youngest dependent child is aged at least 14 years, referred to in clause 1(e), includes, without limitation, any such beneficiary who is—

a beneficiary who (as a person with a dependent child aged under 14 years) was on 15 July 2013 transferred by clause 2(6) and (8) of Schedule 32 of the Social Security Act 1964 to sole parent support that after 14 July 2013 and under section 20H(1) and (2) of that Act expired, and was replaced with jobseeker support that commenced, on the date that the beneficiary's youngest dependent child turned 14 years old; or

a beneficiary who (as a person without a dependent child aged under 14 years) was on 15 July 2013 transferred to jobseeker support by clause 2(9) and (11) of Schedule 32 of the Social Security Act 1964; or
a beneficiary whose entitlement to jobseeker support arose from applying for it, and being granted it, after 14 July 2013.

For the purposes of clause 1(e) and of clause 1(f), MSD may disregard up to $20 a week of the beneficiary’s personal earnings used to meet the cost of childcare for any of the beneficiary’s dependent children.

A person to whom section 30(e) (which relates to having lost the regular support of a spouse or partner who is subject to a sentence of imprisonment, etc) applies is treated as a single beneficiary for the purposes of clauses 1(e) and (f) and 4.
In this Part, dependent child (as defined in Schedule 2) excludes a child in respect of whom an orphan’s benefit or an unsupported child’s benefit is being paid.
In this Part, living with a parent, in relation to a person and a parent (which, in this definition, means a parent, a guardian, or a person acting as a parent),—
means living in the same home as a parent of the person; but
does not include a situation in which a person lives in the same home as a parent of the person if—
the parent is financially dependent on the person; or
the person, at the person’s own expense, provides accommodation for the parent in the home; or
the person provides a substantial degree of care to the parent; or
the home is a hostel, boardinghouse, or similar lodging, and the person and the parent pay for accommodation in it.

part.heading: Sole parent support

legtable.summary: The following table is small in size and has 2 columns. The columns have no headings.

para.text: To a beneficiary with 1 or more dependent children: $521.52 a week, subject to Income Test 1.
For the purposes of clause 1, MSD may disregard up to $20 a week of the beneficiary's personal earnings used to meet the cost of childcare for any of the beneficiary's dependent children.

For the purposes of clause 1, MSD may treat all or any part of the income (including, without limitation, any pension or allowance under the Veterans' Support Act 2014) of the spouse or partner of an applicant to whom section 30(b) or (e) applies as if it were income derived by that applicant if MSD—
has had regard to its nature and the means by which it was derived; and
considers that treatment of it to be reasonable in the circumstances.
In this Part, dependent child (as defined in Schedule 2) excludes a child in respect of whom an orphan’s benefit or an unsupported child’s benefit is being paid.

part.heading: Supported living payment

row.entry: To a single beneficiary under the age of 18 years without dependent children
$355.87 a week, subject to Income Test 1
To any other single beneficiary without dependent children
$424.60 a week, subject to Income Test 1
To a single beneficiary with 1 or more dependent children
$581.96 a week, subject to Income Test 1
To a beneficiary who is in a relationship, whose spouse or partner is granted, in the spouse’s or partner’s own right, a main benefit under this Act
$360.72 a week subject to Income Test 2 if the beneficiary and his or her spouse or partner do not have 1 or more dependent children; or
$378.47 a week subject to Income Test 2 if the beneficiary and his or her spouse or partner have 1 or more dependent children
To a beneficiary who is in a relationship, whose spouse or partner is granted, in the spouse’s or partner’s own right, New Zealand superannuation or a veteran’s pension
$360.72 a week subject to Income Test 2 if the beneficiary and his or her spouse or partner do not have 1 or more dependent children; or
$392.94 a week subject to Income Test 2 if the beneficiary and his or her spouse or partner have 1 or more dependent children

To a beneficiary receiving a supported living payment under section 34 on the ground of restricted work capacity or total blindness who is in a relationship, whose spouse or partner is not granted, in the spouse’s or partner’s own right, a main benefit under this Act or New Zealand superannuation or a veteran’s pension
$721.44 a week subject to Income Test 1 if the beneficiary and his or her spouse or partner do not have 1 or more dependent children; or
$756.94 a week subject to Income Test 1 if the beneficiary and his or her spouse or partner have 1 or more dependent children

To a beneficiary receiving a supported living payment under section 40 on the ground of caring for another person, if the beneficiary is in a relationship, and the beneficiary’s spouse or partner is not granted, in the spouse’s or partner’s own right, a main benefit under this Act or New Zealand superannuation or a veteran’s pension
$360.72 a week subject to Income Test 1 if the beneficiary and his or her spouse or partner do not have 1 or more dependent children; or
$392.94 a week subject to Income Test 1 if the beneficiary and his or her spouse or partner have 1 or more dependent children
However, in computing the income of any beneficiary receiving a supported living payment under section 34 for the purpose of this schedule, MSD must—
disregard that part of the beneficiary's income (not exceeding $20 a week) earned by the beneficiary's own efforts; and
disregard all of the income of a totally blind beneficiary earned by the beneficiary’s own efforts.
Maximum amount from all sources where subsidy (that is to say, an additional allowance under clause 3) is paid on earnings of a blind beneficiary, in every case

entry.insertwords: $581.89 a week, increased by $75.32 a week if the beneficiary is a single person

row.entry: A person (P) who receives a supported living payment in respect of P’s total blindness and who is employed, is entitled to an additional allowance (subsidy) equal to 25% of P’s average earnings for the year.

However, if payment of that additional allowance would result in the total amount P receives from all sources for P’s own use in any year, including the value of any benefits in kind, exceeding the appropriate amount specified in clause 2, the additional allowance—
must be paid at a reduced rate to avoid that result.
A beneficiary who is in a relationship is entitled to be paid at half the appropriate rate in clause 1(d), (e), (g), or (h) and at half the abatement rate applying to that rate if the beneficiary—
has a psychiatric, intellectual, physical, or mental disability; and
is receiving long-term residential care in a hospital or rest home because of that disability; and
has not been means assessed under Part 6 of the Residential Care and Disability Support Services Act 2018.

A supported living payment payable to the spouse or partner of a person to whom clause 5 applies is payable at the appropriate rate specified in clause 1(b) or (c) if that spouse or partner is not receiving long-term residential care in a hospital or rest home.

Despite paragraphs (d), (e), and (g) of clause 1, the rate of a supported living payment on the ground of caring for another person under any of those paragraphs must not be less than the rates that would be payable if the beneficiary and the spouse or partner of the beneficiary were both entitled to receive the benefit on those grounds; but the rate of benefit payable by virtue of this clause must not exceed—
$360.72 a week if the beneficiary and the spouse or partner of the beneficiary have no dependent children; or
$392.94 a week if the beneficiary and the spouse or partner of the beneficiary have 1 or more dependent children.
In this Part, dependent child (as defined in Schedule 2) excludes a child in respect of whom an orphan’s benefit or an unsupported child’s benefit is being paid.

part.heading: Orphan’s benefit

legtable.summary: The following table is small in size and contains 4 columns. The columns have no headings.

row.entry: In respect of a child aged 14 years or over
In respect of a child aged over 10 years and under 14 years
In respect of a child aged over 5 years and under 10 years
In respect of a child aged under 5 years

The amount specified in clause 1 must be diminished by $1 for every complete $1 of the annual income of the child (other than personal earnings of the child) in excess of 50% of the appropriate amount specified under that clause.
The following allowances in respect of the child:
on and after 1 July 2018, a clothing allowance; and
on and after 1 December 2021, an annual holiday allowance; and
on and after 1 December 2021, a birthday allowance for the child’s birthday in 2022 and every year after that.
The allowances are in the same amount or at the same rate as the rates of payment determined under section 363(1) of the Oranga Tamariki Act 1989 for—
any Christmas allowance (or any subsequent annual holiday allowance):
The allowances are in addition to the applicable rate in clause 1.

entry.emphasis: When caregiver is entitled to and paid holiday allowance

row.entry: A person is entitled to the holiday allowance if the person is entitled to the orphan’s benefit for the child on 25 December.
The holiday allowance is payable before 25 December to a person whom MSD expects to be entitled to that allowance.

Payment of the holiday allowance before 25 December is subject to the condition that the person is liable to repay the whole of the allowance if the person is not entitled to the allowance on 25 December.

entry.emphasis: When caregiver is entitled to and paid birthday allowance

row.entry: A person is entitled to the birthday allowance if the person is entitled to the orphan’s benefit for the child on the entitlement date.
The entitlement date for the birthday allowance is the date of the child’s birthday.
However, 1 March is the entitlement date for a birthday that occurs, or would otherwise occur, on 29 February.
The birthday allowance is payable before the entitlement date to a person whom MSD expects to be entitled to that allowance.

Payment of the birthday allowance before the entitlement date is subject to the condition that the person is liable to repay the whole of the allowance if the person is not entitled to the allowance on that date.
For the purposes of this Part, a person is considered to be entitled to the orphan’s benefit for the child if the person is being paid that benefit for the child under section 329(b).

Payment of the birthday allowance before the entitlement date is subject to the condition that the person is liable to repay the whole of the allowance if the person is not entitled to the allowance on that date.
For the purposes of this Part, a person is considered to be entitled to the unsupported child’s benefit for the child if the person is being paid that benefit for the child under section 329(b).

legtable.summary: The following table is small in size and has 3 columns. The columns have no headings.

row.entry: $324.50 a week subject to clauses 11 and 11A

para.text: For a single young person who—
is aged 16 to 17 years; and
is living with or being financially supported by a parent or guardian; and
has a dependent child or children

row.entry: $300.86 a week subject to clauses 11 and 11A

para.text: For any other single young person with a dependent child or children

row.entry: $521.52 a week subject to clauses 11 and 11A

para.text: For a young person who is in a relationship and is without dependent children

row.entry: $316.97 a week subject to clauses 12 and 12A

para.text: For a young person who is in a relationship and has a dependent child or children

row.entry: $334.70 a week subject to clauses 12 and 12A if the young person’s spouse or partner is not receiving a main benefit under this Act; or
$334.70 a week subject to clauses 12 and 12A if the young person’s spouse or partner is receiving a main benefit under this Act

para.text: In this Part, dependent child (as defined in Schedule 2) excludes a child in respect of whom an orphan’s benefit or an unsupported child’s benefit is being paid.

legtable.summary: The following table is small in size and has 2 columns. The columns have no headings.

para.text: Maximum amount of in-hand allowance

legtable.summary: The following table is small in size and has 3 columns. The columns have no headings.

para.text: Education, training, or work-based learning incentive
Budgeting incentive
Parenting education incentive

legtable.summary: The following table is small in size and has 2 columns. The columns have no headings.

para.text: The applicable rate of youth payment or young parent payment in clause 1, 2, or 3 must be reduced by $1 for every $1 of the young person’s weekly income (excluding any 1 or more information share child support payments) that exceeds $284.70, but no amount of youth payment or young parent payment or incentive payment is payable if the young person’s weekly income (excluding any 1 or more information share child support payments) exceeds $334.70.

If, after complying with clause 11, an amount of youth payment or young parent payment or incentive payment is payable, the applicable rate of youth payment or young parent payment in clause 1, 2, or 3 must also be reduced by $1 for every $1 of any of the young person’s relevant weekly income (as defined in clause 11B).
In clause 11A, the young person’s relevant weekly income means their weekly income—
that is 1 or more information share child support payments; and
that exceeds $284.70 of their weekly income that is 1 or both of—
any weekly income (excluding any 1 or more information share child support payments):
any 1 or more information share child support payments.

The applicable rate of youth payment or young parent payment in clause 4 or 5 must be reduced by 50 cents for every $1 of the combined weekly income of the young person and the young person’s spouse or partner (excluding any 1 or more information share child support payments) that exceeds $284.70, but no amount of youth payment or young parent payment or incentive payment is payable if that combined weekly income (excluding any 1 or more information share child support payments) exceeds $384.70.

If, after complying with clause 12, an amount of youth payment or young parent payment or incentive payment is payable, the applicable rate of youth payment or young parent payment in clause 4 or 5 must also be reduced by 50 cents for every $1 of the relevant combined weekly income of the young person and the young person’s spouse or partner (as defined in clause 12B) that exceeds $284.70.
In clause 12A, the relevant combined weekly income of the young person and the young person’s spouse or partner means their combined weekly income—
that is 1 or more information share child support payments; and
that exceeds $284.70 of their combined weekly income that is 1 or both of—
any weekly income (excluding any 1 or more information share child support payments):
any 1 or more information share child support payments.

For the purposes of clause 11, 11A, 12, or 12A and a rate of young parent payment, MSD may disregard up to $20 a week of the personal earnings of the young person (excluding any that are the young person’s weekly income that is 1 or more information share child support payments) used to meet the costs of childcare for any of the person’s dependent children (see clause 6).

para.text: In this Part,—
accommodation income, for a person (P) and a period, means the sum of—

all rent paid to P for the period by other persons (not being additional residents or boarders) residing at the premises at which P resides in consideration of the right to occupy those premises or a non-self-contained part of those premises (excluding any service costs, any arrears, and any rent that is income from a business carried on by P); and
62% of all contributions received by P for the period from additional residents residing at the premises at which P resides (excluding any arrears); and

62% of the total amount of all payments for board and lodgings at those premises made to P for the period by boarders residing at those premises (excluding any arrears and any payments that are income from a business carried on by P)
dependent child (as defined in Schedule 2) excludes a child in respect of whom an orphan’s benefit or an unsupported child’s benefit is being paid
weekly qualifying accommodation costs, for a person, means the greater of zero and the amount calculated in accordance with the following formula:
where—
is the person’s weekly accommodation costs
is,—
if the person does not use any separately identifiable part of the premises at which they reside primarily for business purposes, zero; or
if paragraph (a) does not apply, the sum of the areas of each separately identifiable part of the premises at which the person resides that the person uses primarily for business purposes
is,—
if the person does not let any self-contained part of the premises at which they reside to another person or other persons who reside at those premises, zero; or

if paragraph (a) does not apply, the sum of the areas of each self-contained part of the premises at which the person resides that the person lets to another person or other persons who reside at those premises
is,—
if the person does not let any separately identifiable part of the premises at which they reside to another person or other persons who do not reside at those premises, zero; or

if paragraph (a) does not apply, the sum of the areas of each separately identifiable part of the premises at which the person resides that the person lets to another person or other persons who do not reside at those premises
is the total area of the premises at which the person resides
is the person’s weekly accommodation income.
